creator: FreeBSD@ShaneWare.Biz
text:
(In reply to Thomas Zander from comment #8)
> (In reply to FreeBSD from comment #7)
> > I can't say that is the first time I have seen that happen. While the size
> > and commit checksums mismatch, diff doesn't find any file changes between
> > the old tarball and the current one.
> 
> I just took a look at github.
> Commit 9a93777 is marked 0.9.6 release while the newer commit 71ec7c5 has
> the log entry "Version bump to 0.9.6.1", see
> https://github.com/hydrogen-music/hydrogen/commits/0.9.6
> So some change in tarball content and size is expected.
> 
> On a side note: In fact it would be a desaster for the whole cryptographic
> commit chain argument in git if it was possible to just change commit IDs on
> existing commits without changing some content (incl meta-info) as well.
> 
> > The updated patch differs only in the distinfo and GH_COMMIT

At first I thought the tag had been deleted and re-created, but now I think it is github changing again (or is it the ports download step?), I have another port with a checksum mismatch.

The GH_COMMIT is added to the untared folder name. The **realy** good thing - download hydrogen 0.9.6 and 0.9.6.1 - they both untar with the same folder suffix but have differing file contents.

I have made some other changes with the 0.9.6.1 update - removed libarchive and oss options and added pulseaudio. All the test builds haven't finished yet, so was waiting before submitting.

--

The error relates to libarchive, the port version(3.1.2) hasn't changed in over 12 months. In the build log at the end of configure it prints info on the config - any chance that step shows libarchive not used or not found? Which should give a file not found for the archive.h.
